what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG icon showing an L-shaped measuring corner with tick marks and an internal m2 label.
observations: Uses a 24x24 viewBox with no fill and currentColor stroke.
Draws the measuring frame and tick marks with three path elements.
Uses round stroke caps and joins with stroke width 1.5.
Places the unit label using two SVG text elements: m and 2.
The text elements use currentColor fill and no stroke.
standouts: Combines ruler-like axes with an area unit label inside the icon.
Uses SVG text alongside path-based linework.
conventions: Follows common inline icon conventions: xmlns, viewBox, fill="none", stroke="currentColor", rounded line caps and joins.
Keeps geometry compact in a single-line SVG.
Breaks from purely path-based icon construction by including text elements.
concerns: The SVG has no title, desc, role, or aria attributes within the file.
The unit label depends on SVG text rendering rather than path outlines.
notes: This is a square-meter area icon built from ruler corner linework plus an m2 text label.
questions: Should the text label remain live SVG text, or should it be converted to paths for consistent rendering?
Is accessibility metadata expected to be provided by the SVG itself or by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename implies an area-m2 icon, and the file depicts a measuring-corner symbol labeled m2.
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate floor or surface area measurement in square meters within a compact design-system icon.
novelty: The distinct element is the embedded m2 label inside an otherwise stroke-based ruler icon.
contribution: Contributes a compact square-meter measurement glyph that combines spatial measurement cues with an explicit unit label.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG icon of an L-shaped ruler corner with tick marks and an m2 label for area measurement.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for representing area in square meters as a reusable design-system icon.
